rationale: Both candidates independently rediscover the async_chunk default-inconsistency issue (echoing the GT Low comment), but Y's version is sharper — it cites the exact sibling default (line 3823) and the real OmniModelConfig field default, while X's is a plausible but less precise cross-file comparison. X pads its findings with several weak 'please re-run this test' asks that are grounded but low-value and dilute precision. Y is the only one to explicitly question whether the split-by-total_scheduled_tokens logic still handles tensors aligned to hidden_states.shape[0], correctly anticipating the multi-axis gap later fixed by PR #4910 — satisfying the LATENT GAP CHECK, which X never touches. Neither candidate recovers the amy-why-3459 'move to model' design question or explicitly the original (already-fixed) Med gating concern, capping recall for both.
